'''Bradley Meyers''' (born 5 January 1980), also known by the [[nickname]]s of '''"Big Red"''',<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,23739,23457084-10389,00.html|work=The Courier-Mail|title=Big Red Brad Meyers back in top grade|first=Steve|last=Ricketts|date=30 March 2008}}</ref> or '''"Two Step"''',<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/injury-forces-titans-big-man-meyers-into-retirement-20110509-1ef1b.html|title=Injury forces Titans big man Meyers into retirement}}</ref> is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. A [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] and [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian]] national representative forward, he played his club football in the [[National Rugby League]] for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]], and in the [[Super League]] for the [[Bradford Bulls]], with whom he won 2005's [[Super League X]] Championship.

==Background==
Meyers attended school at [[Villanova College (Australia)|Villanova College]] where he initially played [[rugby union]]{{Citation needed|date=April 2009}}. While attending Villanova College, Meyers played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref> While with the [[Brisbane Broncos]], he also played for the [[Past Brothers]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>[http://queensland.rleague.com/results/1998/round-22.php Round 22: 15, 16 August] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080517203353/http://queensland.rleague.com/results/1998/round-22.php|date=17 May 2008}} ''queensland.rleague.com''</ref>

==Brisbane Broncos==
Having won the [[2000 NRL Premiership]], the [[Brisbane Broncos]] traveled to England to play against 2000's [[Super League V]] Champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.]] for the [[2001 World Club Challenge]], with Meyers playing from the interchange bench and scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in Brisbane's loss. He made his début for the Broncos in the Australian National Rugby League competition and later was picked for the [[Rugby League State of Origin|State of Origin]] series in Australia where he represented the [[Queensland Rugby League team|Queensland Maroons]] in their triumph over their fierce rivals the [[New South Wales Rugby League team|New South Wales Blues]]. The big redhead then went on to represent [[Australian national rugby league team|Australia]] in two tests against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] on the [[2001 Kangaroo tour]]. He played 102 games for the Brisbane Broncos before signing for the Bradford Bulls in 2005.

==Bradford Bulls==
Meyers had two seasons at the [[Bradford Bulls]] and played in the [[2005 Super League grand final]]-winning team that beat Leeds 15–8. As [[Super League]] champions Bradford faced [[National Rugby League]] premiers [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Meyers played at second-row forward in the Bulls' 30-10 victory.

==Gold Coast Titans==
On 1 July 2006, it was announced that the new NRL franchise, the [[Gold Coast Titans]], had signed Meyers on a two-year deal. Meyers stated, "I am really looking forward to playing with the Titans. It's a new challenge for my career and I couldn't think of a better place to be."<ref name="Titans confirm Meyers signing">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/m/meyers_brad/index.php|title=Rleague.com - Meyers, Brad|accessdate=2006-07-30|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20060510105610/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/m/meyers_brad/index.php|archivedate=10 May 2006}} "Titans confirm Meyers signing" article. URL retrieved 30 July 2006.</ref> He did however explain that he "really enjoyed playing here at Bradford. It was a change I needed but now it is time to head back home. I have a few goals I wish to achieve within the [[NRL]] and feel that these last couple of years have given me a solid background on which to make my return."<ref name="Titans confirm Meyers signing"/> In May 2011, Meyers announced that would be his last year in the [[NRL]].

Myers only managed to play two games for the Gold Coast in the [[2011 NRL season]] as the club finished last and claimed the [[Wooden spoon (award)|wooden spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-defeat-the-gold-coast-titans-to-avoid-wooden-spoon-for-2011/news-story/fc87216a6a23a4b8d0eefd6174b60c47/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat the Gold Coast Titans to avoid wooden spoon for 2011|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}}</ref>

==Personal==
Brad is an Australian Apprenticeships Ambassador for the Australian Government.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.australianapprenticeships.gov.au/ambassador/brad-meyers |title=Love for outdoors leads to carpentry: Brad Meyers | Australian Apprenticeships |access-date=11 January 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160307173125/http://www.australianapprenticeships.gov.au/ambassador/brad-meyers |archive-date=7 March 2016 |url-status=dead }}</ref>
